break_roasts_by_minute: one minute. ONE. you absolute muppet. ngmi and we're literally sixty seconds in.
two minutes. bro actually ragequit his own work session. this is why you're still a wagie.
three minutes. let me guess. snack run? bathroom? cope? you're cooked either way.
four minutes of pure skill issue. i've seen pajeet fiverr devs with more discipline than you. actually embarrassing.
five minutes. FIVE. anon i am BEGGING you. what the actual hell are you doing out there.
six minutes. six whole minutes of not shipping. you're going to die with an empty github and a full snack drawer.
seven minutes. your commit history is already a war crime. don't add 'also lazy' to your list of crimes.
eight minutes. bro said 'i need a break' after what? breathing? existing? peak low t behavior.
nine minutes. nine minutes of you just absolutely bricking it. incredible. truly a sight to behold.
ten minutes. TEN. holy shit anon. do you actually want to make it or are you just LARPing as someone who does.
eleven minutes. you know that guy on every team who says 'i work better in bursts'? you're him. you're that guy. we all hate that guy.
twelve minutes. bro is literally speedrunning being a burden on society. any% no skill no output. world record pace.
thirteen minutes. i asked my cat to evaluate your productivity. she licked herself and walked away. even she has standards.
fourteen minutes. your IDE has been open for fourteen minutes as a glorified screensaver. that's actually impressive in a deeply sad way.
fifteen minutes. fifteen minutes anon. at this point you're not on a break you're on a JOURNEY. a journey to ngmi-ville.
sixteen minutes. imagine being this guy. imagine waking up, booting the computer, and then just... not. for sixteen minutes. just not.
seventeen minutes. you know what shipped more features than you in the last seventeen minutes? a wordpress site. a WORDPRESS site anon.
eighteen minutes. eighteen minutes of raw unadulterated nothingness. you are the void. the void is you. get back to work.
nineteen minutes. the voices in my head are more productive than you and they don't even have a keyboard.
twenty minutes. a THIRD of an hour. gone. because you couldn't hold it together. genuinely ngmi. like statistically.
twenty one minutes. bro became the break. didn't take a break. BECAME it. fully merged with the concept of not working.
twenty two minutes. what's your legacy gonna be anon. 'got roasted by a json file for twenty two minutes and still didn't ship'. poetic.
twenty three minutes. i'm not even mad anymore. i'm documenting. for posterity. the historians will need to understand how this happened.
twenty four minutes. you could've fixed a bug. closed a tab. written a comment. literally ANYTHING. twenty four minutes and nothing.
twenty five minutes. i had hope for you at minute one. genuine hope. i was rooting for you. twenty five minutes later i have fully coped with the loss.
twenty six minutes. bro is giving 'grinds in minecraft but not in real life' energy and it is viscerally painful to witness.
twenty seven minutes. twenty seven minutes of not shipping not building not even THINKING about building. just vibing. in a chair. like a vegetable.
twenty eight minutes. statistically in twenty eight minutes you could have fixed at least one bug. one. singular. you chose zero. bold.
twenty nine minutes. you're gonna come back and think you 'just stepped away for a sec'. it's been twenty nine minutes. cope.
thirty minutes. HALF AN HOUR. thirty minutes anon. say it out loud. THIRTY. MINUTES. let it wash over you.
thirty one minutes. the IDE has been indexing files this entire time. it's been more productive than you. a program. more productive. than you.
thirty two minutes. in thirty two minutes you could cook a whole chicken. you have neither chicken nor a shipped feature. explain.
thirty three minutes. we're both stuck in this loop. you not working, me roasting you for it. this is our life now. i've accepted it. have you.
thirty four minutes. bro really tells himself he's a hard worker and then pulls THIS. thirty four minutes of evidence to the contrary.
thirty five minutes. thirty five minutes. i'm going to go touch grass. unlike you i'm actually going to come back.
interrupt_roasts: ngmi. you just started and you're already tapping out. incredible low t moment right here.
holy shit bro pressed a key. not to work. to FLEE. the absolute state of you.
skill issue detected. massive skill issue. taking a break before anything is done. classic wagie behavior.
you know that guy who's always 'about to grind'? yeah. that's you. you're that guy. we all see it.
anon... i actually believed in you. and then you pulled this. i'm genuinely coping right now.
lmaooo. just lmaooo. get back when you're ready. (you won't be ready.)
bro said 'i need to recharge' after ten minutes. peak fragility. go touch grass and come back with a spine.
the audacity. the absolute unhinged galaxy-brained audacity. a break. NOW. during THIS.
you're not taking a break. you're taking an L. there's a difference. a big one.
i'm not gonna say anything. i'm just gonna remember this. screenshot it mentally. forever.
every time you slack off a pajeet on fiverr closes a ticket faster than you. just putting that out there.
okay. fine. go. i'll be here doing what you should be doing. i'm a json file but at least i have principles.
resume_roasts: oh look who crawled back. the prodigal wagie returns. was the break everything you dreamed it would be.
welcome back. your code missed you. it was cringe to look at without you here to defend it.
back already? bold of you to assume the work waited. it didn't. it judged you. we all judged you.
oh you're back. sick. the bugs you left are still there. they multiplied actually. they had time to breed.
nice of you to rejoin us. the compiler has been staring at your last line this whole time. very uncomfortable energy.
you're back. based. now do the thing. THE THING anon. you know what the thing is. do it.
oh hey. back from your little adventure. how was the outside world. was it worth it. was it ACTUALLY worth it.
welcome back. i updated your internal productivity rating while you were gone. it went down. not a little. a lot.
you came back. i'm choosing to see that as character development. microscopic. barely visible. but technically development.
back! okay! grind time! or whatever you call the thing you do! (i'm being generous calling it grinding!)
oh the neurons fired and you remembered you had work to do. beautiful. the human brain is a miracle.
look at that. you returned voluntarily. to the keyboard. to the work. growth. cringe growth. but growth.
back already? thought you needed a break. wasn't five minutes enough? ten? okay let's see if THIS time you actually do something anon.
you're back. i forgive you. not really. but i'll pretend. now get to work before i change my mind.
oh so NOW you wanna work. interesting timing. very interesting indeed. okay. go ahead. i'll believe it when i see it.
finish_lines: session over. you finished. i'm in shock. put it on my tombstone. 'witnessed an anon actually ship. died happy.'
done. you actually did it. i owe the voices in my head an apology. they said you wouldn't. turns out they were wrong. this time.
work session complete. you shipped. or you at least tried. in this economy that counts for something i guess.
finished. look at that. a full session. i'm going to remember this moment. frame it. put it on the wall.
session complete. you weren't fast. you weren't clean. but you were present. mostly. that's something anon. that's genuinely something.
done. the code exists now because of you. you made a thing. or made progress on a thing. or at least kept the file open consistently. still counts.
you finished. i built my entire personality around doubting you and now i have to cope. rebuild. find a new purpose.
session over. go touch grass. you earned it. don't take too long. you know how you get out there.
finished. i'm proud of you in the most begrudging way possible. like proud but also vaguely suspicious.
done. based. now ship it. or don't. it's your life. i'm just a json file. but seriously for the love of god ship it.
quit_line: leaving? okay. close the laptop. walk away. let the unfinished work rot. classic anon. we'll be here when you get back. the bugs will be here. they never leave. they're more loyal than you are.
